title: Set up pre-commit hooks with Husky and lint-staged
description:
Add pre-commit hooks to automatically format and lint code before commits. Ensures code quality and consistency.

Installation:
npm install --save-dev husky lint-staged

Setup:
1. Initialize husky: npx husky install
2. Add pre-commit hook: npx husky add .husky/pre-commit 'npx lint-staged'
3. Configure lint-staged in package.json

Configuration (package.json):
{
  'lint-staged': {
    '*.{ts,tsx}': ['eslint --fix', 'prettier --write'],
    '*.{css,md,json}': ['prettier --write']
  }
}

Benefits:
- Auto-format on commit
- Catch lint errors early
- Consistent code style
- Prevent bad commits
- Team-friendly workflow

Test by making a commit with intentional formatting issues

title: Consolidate duplicate ChartCard components
description:
Two ChartCard implementations exist (similar to StatCard issue). Consolidate into one.

Current files:
- src/components/common/ChartCard.tsx (basic wrapper)
- src/components/analytics/ChartCard.tsx (enhanced with filters, export)

Solution:
Create unified component in src/components/common/ChartCard.tsx with:
- title, description props
- optional filters area (slot/children)
- optional export button
- loading state
- empty state
- error state
- custom actions area

Benefits:
- Reduced duplication
- Consistent chart wrapper across app
- Easier to maintain
- Smaller bundle

Update all chart components to use unified ChartCard

title: Add skeleton loading states across pages
description:
Replace spinners with skeleton UI for better perceived performance and professional UX.

Create components:
- src/components/common/SkeletonCard.tsx
- src/components/common/SkeletonTable.tsx
- src/components/common/SkeletonChart.tsx
- src/components/common/SkeletonStat.tsx

Implementation:
- Use animate-pulse with gray-200 backgrounds
- Match actual content layout
- Show during isLoading state

Update pages:
- HomePage (stat cards)
- CharactersPage (table)
- Analytics pages (charts + stats)
- Detail pages

Example:
{isLoading ? (
  <SkeletonCard />
) : (
  <StatCard data={data} />
)}

title: Epic: Critical Fixes - Must Fix Immediately
description: Critical issues that must be fixed before continuing development. These affect production readiness and core functionality.

title: Add toast notification system
description:
Implement toast notifications for user feedback on actions (chart downloads, errors, etc).

Options:
1. Use react-hot-toast (lightweight, 3KB)
2. Build custom toast component

Recommendation: Use react-hot-toast for speed

Implementation:
1. npm install react-hot-toast
2. Add <Toaster /> to App.tsx
3. Replace console feedback with toasts

Use cases:
- Chart export success/failure
- Data fetch errors
- Search errors
- Future: Form submissions

Examples:
- toast.success('Chart downloaded successfully!')
- toast.error('Failed to load data. Please try again.')
- toast.loading('Exporting chart...')

Configure position: top-center or bottom-right

title: Add React Error Boundary component
description:
Create an ErrorBoundary component to catch React errors and prevent full app crashes.

Implementation:
1. Create src/components/common/ErrorBoundary.tsx
2. Implement componentDidCatch lifecycle
3. Add error logging via logger utility
4. Create user-friendly error UI with retry button
5. Wrap App routes in ErrorBoundary

Example:
<ErrorBoundary>
  <Suspense fallback={<LoadingSpinner />}>
    <Routes>...</Routes>
  </Suspense>
</ErrorBoundary>

References: React docs on Error Boundaries

title: Improve error messaging UI
description:
Add user-friendly error states with clear messaging and recovery options. Currently errors are minimal.

Create components:
- src/components/common/ErrorState.tsx
- src/components/common/EmptyState.tsx

ErrorState features:
- Error icon
- User-friendly message (not technical details)
- Retry button
- Optional support link

EmptyState features:
- Icon
- Helpful message
- Call-to-action button

Use in:
- All pages with data fetching
- Search results (no matches)
- Filtered views (no results)

Example:
{error ? (
  <ErrorState error={error} onRetry={refetch} />
) : data.length === 0 ? (
  <EmptyState title='No characters found' action={<button>Clear Filters</button>} />
) : (
  <Content data={data} />
)}

title: Split analyticsService.ts into domain-specific services
description:
analyticsService.ts is 23KB with 20+ query functions. Split into smaller domain-specific services for better organization and code splitting.

Current file:
- src/services/analyticsService.ts (23KB, 20+ functions)

New structure:
src/services/analytics/
├── bountyAnalytics.ts (bounty-related queries)
├── appearanceAnalytics.ts (character appearances)
├── timelineAnalytics.ts (temporal analysis)
├── arcAnalytics.ts (arc/saga statistics)
├── demographicAnalytics.ts (age, origin, etc)
└── index.ts (barrel export)

Benefits:
- Smaller file sizes for better code splitting
- Easier to locate specific queries
- Better tree-shaking
- Improved maintainability
- Domain-based organization

Update imports in analytics pages

title: Refactor Navigation.tsx - Extract dropdown components
description:
Navigation.tsx is 432 lines (18KB) with repeated dropdown logic. Split into smaller, reusable components.

Current issue:
- Large monolithic component
- Repeated dropdown patterns
- Hard to test
- Poor code reusability

New structure:
src/components/navigation/
├── Navigation.tsx (main orchestrator, <100 lines)
├── NavDropdown.tsx (reusable dropdown wrapper)
├── NavSection.tsx (story, media, analytics sections)
├── MobileMenu.tsx (mobile drawer)
├── MobileSearch.tsx (mobile search modal)
└── constants.ts (navigation config data)

Benefits:
- Better code splitting
- Easier to test
- Reusable dropdown logic
- Improved readability
- Smaller bundle chunks

Update imports in Header.tsx and App.tsx

title: Fix Vite base path for GitHub Pages deployment
Improve accessibility - Add ARIA labels and keyboard nav
description:
Enhance accessibility to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance (project goal per CLAUDE.md).

Tasks:
1. Add ARIA labels to complex components:
   - Charts: role='img' with aria-label describing data
   - Tables: Add <caption> for screen readers
   - Loading states: aria-live='polite' announcements
   
2. Keyboard navigation improvements:
   - Ensure all interactive elements are focusable
   - Test tab order is logical
   - Add skip-to-content link
   - Ensure modals trap focus properly
   
3. Screen reader testing:
   - Test with NVDA (Windows) or VoiceOver (Mac)
   - Verify all content is announced
   - Check dynamic content updates
   
4. Focus management:
   - Visible focus indicators on all elements
   - Focus returns to trigger after modal close
   
5. Color contrast audit:
   - Install @axe-core/react for automated testing
   - Fix any contrast issues found

Files to update: All page components, charts, tables, modals

title: Add URL state for filters (shareable links)
description:
Filter state is currently local only. Make filters shareable via URL using search params.

Benefits:
- Shareable filtered views
- Bookmarkable states
- Browser back/forward works
- Better UX

Implementation:
Use React Router's useSearchParams hook

Example (CharactersPage):
const [searchParams, setSearchParams] = useSearchParams()
const status = searchParams.get('status') || 'all'
const sort = searchParams.get('sort') || 'name'

const handleFilterChange = (newStatus) => {
  setSearchParams({ status: newStatus, sort })
}

Pages to update:
- CharactersPage (status, affiliation filters)
- ArcsPage (saga filter)
- Analytics pages (time ranges, filters)

Considerations:
- Default values when no params
- Encode special characters
- Validate param values
- Clear params on 'clear filters' action

Test by sharing URLs with filters applied

title: Add JSDoc comments to service functions
description:
Service functions lack documentation. Add JSDoc comments for better developer experience and IDE support.

Template:
/**
 * Fetches bounty distribution data grouped by ranges.
 * 
 * @returns Promise resolving to bounty distribution with color-coded ranges
 * @throws {Error} If Supabase query fails
 * 
 * @example
 * const data = await fetchBountyDistribution()
 * // Returns: [{ range: '0-100M', count: 45, color: '#3b82f6' }, ...]
 */

Files to document:
- src/services/characterService.ts
- src/services/arcService.ts
- src/services/searchService.ts
- src/services/statsService.ts
- src/services/analyticsService.ts (or split services)
- src/services/chapterService.ts
- src/services/volumeService.ts
- src/services/sagaService.ts

Include:
- Function purpose
- Parameters (if any)
- Return type description
- Error handling
- Usage examples

title: Epic: Architecture Improvements - Refactoring & Optimization
description: Refactoring large components, splitting services, and optimizing performance. These improve maintainability and scalability.

title: Optimize FontAwesome imports for bundle size
description:
Ensure tree-shakeable imports to avoid bundling entire icon library.

Current risk:
Importing entire icon sets adds significant bundle size

Correct pattern:
// ❌ Bad - imports entire library
import { fas } from '@fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons'

// ✅ Good - import specific icons only
import { faUser, faBook, faChartBar } from '@fortawesome/free-solid-svg-icons'

Tasks:
1. Audit all FontAwesome imports
2. Replace bulk imports with specific icon imports
3. Create icon registry file if many icons used:
   src/utils/iconRegistry.ts
4. Measure bundle size before/after
5. Document icon usage pattern

Tools:
- npm run build (check dist/assets/*.js sizes)
- webpack-bundle-analyzer (if needed)

Expected impact:
Reduce bundle by 50-200KB depending on current usage

Files to check:
- All component files using icons
- Navigation, Header, StatCard, etc.

title: Extract magic numbers and strings to constants
description:
Hard-coded values scattered throughout codebase. Centralize for easier maintenance and consistency.

Create constants files:
src/constants/
├── config.ts (app-wide configuration)
├── cache.ts (React Query cache settings)
├── pagination.ts (pagination defaults)
├── search.ts (search configuration)
└── ui.ts (UI constants like debounce times)

Examples:
- Cache times: 5 * 60 * 1000 → CACHE.DEFAULT_STALE_TIME
- Debounce: 500 → SEARCH.DEBOUNCE_MS
- Page size: 15 → PAGINATION.DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE
- Result limits: 10 → SEARCH.MAX_RESULTS_PER_TYPE

Benefits:
- Single source of truth
- Easy to find and modify settings
- Type-safe with const assertions
- Self-documenting code
- Easier testing

Replace hard-coded values across codebase with named constants

title: Refine React Query cache strategy
description:
Current 24-hour staleTime is too aggressive. Users won't see updates for a full day. Implement per-query cache strategy.

Current config (App.tsx):
- staleTime: 24 hours (too long)
- refetchOnWindowFocus: false (should be true for some queries)

New strategy:
1. Default: 5 minutes staleTime, 30 minutes gcTime, refetchOnWindowFocus: true
2. Reference data (arcs, sagas): 24 hours (rarely changes)
3. Live stats: 1 minute (frequently updated)
4. Character data: 5 minutes (moderate updates)

Implementation:
- Update QueryClient default options
- Add custom staleTime per query type
- Document cache strategy in comments

Files to modify:
- src/App.tsx (default config)
- Service files (per-query overrides)

Test by checking data freshness after various time intervals

title: Write component tests for core UI components
description:
Add tests for reusable components to ensure UI behavior and prevent regressions.

Priority components to test:
1. StatCard - Both variants, all themes, with/without trends
2. ChartCard - With/without filters, export button
3. ErrorState - Display, retry functionality
4. EmptyState - Display, CTA button
5. SkeletonCard/Table/Chart - Render correctly
6. Search - Debouncing, results display, keyboard nav
7. Navigation - Dropdowns, mobile menu, active states

Test patterns:
- Render with different props
- User interactions (clicks, keyboard)
- Conditional rendering (loading, error, empty states)
- Accessibility (ARIA labels, roles)
- Responsive behavior (if critical)

Tools:
- React Testing Library
- @testing-library/user-event for interactions
- @testing-library/jest-dom for assertions

Target: 70%+ coverage of components directory

Create tests for all service functions (high value, easy to test). This provides immediate safety net for refactoring.

Test files to create:

- src/services/__tests__/arcService.test.ts
- src/services/__tests__/searchService.test.ts

Focus on:

Mock Supabase client for isolated testing.

title: Epic: Quality & Polish - Testing, Docs, CI/CD
description: Testing infrastructure, documentation, accessibility improvements, and CI/CD pipeline. These ensure long-term quality and maintainability.

title: Epic: Quick Wins - High Impact, Low Effort
description: High-impact improvements that can be implemented quickly. These improve UX and code quality with minimal risk.

title: Set up Vitest testing infrastructure
description:
Initialize testing framework for the project. Currently no tests exist despite CLAUDE.md claiming 80%+ coverage target.

Setup steps:
1. Verify Vitest is installed (check package.json)
2. Create vitest.config.ts if missing
3. Set up React Testing Library
4. Create test setup file (src/test/setup.ts)
5. Add test script to package.json
6. Create example test to verify setup
7. Document testing patterns in README

Target: Basic infrastructure ready for writing tests

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
description:
Automate testing, linting, and build verification on every push and PR.

Create workflow file:
.github/workflows/ci.yml

Pipeline stages:
1. Install dependencies (npm ci)
2. Run linter (npm run lint)
3. Run tests (npm test)
4. Build production bundle (npm run build)
5. Check bundle size (optional: bundlesize package)

Workflow configuration:
- Trigger on: push, pull_request
- Node version: 20.x (match local development)
- Cache npm dependencies for speed
- Fail fast on first error
- Upload test coverage reports (if using coverage)

Optional enhancements:
- Deploy preview for PRs
- Bundle size tracking
- Lighthouse CI for performance
- Automated dependency updates (Dependabot)

Benefits:
- Catch errors before merge
- Ensure build succeeds
- Automated quality checks
- Confidence in deployments

title: Consolidate duplicate StatCard components
description:
Two StatCard implementations exist with different features. Consolidate into a single flexible component.

Current files:
- src/components/StatCard.tsx (simple, home page)
- src/components/analytics/StatCard.tsx (enhanced, 251 lines)

Solution:
Create unified component with variants:
- variant: 'simple' | 'enhanced'
- theme: 'blue' | 'green' | 'purple' | 'amber' | 'pink' | 'emerald'
- expandable: boolean
- trend: { direction, value }
- icon, tooltip, link support

Benefits:
- Single source of truth
- Reduced bundle size
- Easier maintenance
- Consistent behavior

Update all usages in HomePage and analytics pages.
